sutta: Monks, I know not of any other single thing so quick to change as the mind in so much as it is no easy thing to illustrate how quick to change it is that's all.
commentary: This sutta is telling us that the mind changes extremely fast because it changes extremely fast we should use it to good advantage for example if we have a good intention that comes up like thinking maybe I should become a monk because it is a good intention we should quickly act if we do not act the mind will change the mind is fickle so when there is a good intention we act quickly on the other hand if there is a bad intention we should not act on it for example anger arises if we act on anger we will do something unwholesome because the mind is troubled so instead of acting we do nothing we wait for the anger to subside because the mind changes quickly it will pass so we just wait and do not act this is using the changing nature of mind with wisdom.
